CheckTitle: Detect non-admin users owning applications with high-privilege permissions
Description: Identifies applications requesting high-privilege Graph API permissions where at least one owner is not a privileged administrator. Non-admin app owners can modify credentials on powerful apps, creating privilege escalation paths.
Risk: This was the exact attack vector in the Midnight Blizzard breach: a non-admin user owned a test application that held dangerous permissions. The attacker compromised the non-admin user, added new credentials to the app, and escalated to full tenant compromise.

Remediation.Recommendation: Ensure applications with high-privilege API permissions are owned exclusively by privileged administrators. Remove non-admin owners or reduce the app's permissions to least-privilege.

SourceNotes: Modeled on the Midnight Blizzard attack vector. AI-generated framework mappings.
